title: World Wide Web
content: The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989 while working at CERN. He wrote the first web browser (WorldWideWeb) and web server in 1990. The first website went live on August 6, 1991. Berners-Lee made the technology freely available with no patent and no royalties.

title: Transistor Invention
content: The transistor was invented at Bell Labs in December 1947 by John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, and William Shockley. They demonstrated the first point-contact transistor on December 23, 1947. All three received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1956. The transistor replaced vacuum tubes and enabled modern electronics.

title: First Programmable Computer
content: ENIAC (Electronic Numerical Integrator and Computer) was the first general-purpose electronic digital computer, completed in 1945 at the University of Pennsylvania. It was designed by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert. However, the Colossus computers built in the UK from 1943 were the first programmable electronic digital computers, though they were special-purpose.

title: Moon Landing
content: Apollo 11 was the first crewed mission to land on the Moon. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin landed the lunar module Eagle on July 20, 1969, while Michael Collins orbited above in the command module Columbia. Armstrong was the first person to walk on the Moon, famously saying 'That's one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.'

title: Internet History
content: ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet, sent its first message on October 29, 1969, from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. The message was 'LO' (they were trying to type 'LOGIN' but the system crashed). TCP/IP was adopted as the standard protocol on January 1, 1983, which is considered the birth of the modern Internet.

title: Apple Inc.
content: Apple Computer was founded on April 1, 1976 by Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne. Wayne sold his 10% share back for $800 just 12 days later. The iPhone was announced on January 9, 2007 and released on June 29, 2007. Apple became the first company to reach a $1 trillion market cap on August 2, 2018.

title: GPS System
content: The Global Positioning System (GPS) is operated by the United States Space Force. The first GPS satellite was launched in 1978. The system became fully operational with 24 satellites in 1995. GPS was made freely available for civilian use by President Clinton's directive in 2000 when Selective Availability was turned off, improving civilian accuracy from ~100m to ~10m.

title: Programming Languages
content: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. Java was developed by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems and released in 1995. JavaScript was created by Brendan Eich in 10 days in May 1995 while at Netscape. C was developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs between 1969 and 1973.

title: SpaceX Achievements
content: SpaceX was founded by Elon Musk in 2002. It became the first private company to launch a spacecraft into orbit (Falcon 1, September 28, 2008). The first successful landing of an orbital rocket booster was on December 21, 2015 (Falcon 9). SpaceX's Crew Dragon became the first commercial spacecraft to carry humans to the ISS on May 30, 2020.

title: Artificial Intelligence Milestones
content: The term 'artificial intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy at the 1956 Dartmouth Conference. IBM's Deep Blue defeated chess champion Garry Kasparov on May 11, 1997. Google's AlphaGo defeated Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1 in March 2016. OpenAI released GPT-3 in June 2020 and ChatGPT in November 2022.
